decisionPanels.title: 🚨 Guardian Studios is now your internal client
decisionPanels.summary: The Guardian formed Guardian Studios on 26 Feb 2026, appointing Leah Green as head. Its mandate: transform journalism into compelling new visual formats, reach new audiences, foster on-screen talent. This is the institutional bet on video-first journalism — and MMR is the technical substrate that will either make or break it. Green comes from Channel 4 (Dispatches, Unreported World). Expect a significant uplift in demand for fast ingest, flexible collections, and multi-format output from the multimedia team.
actions: Treat Guardian Studios as an explicit stakeholder for MMR workflow design — especially ingest speed and collection management.
Map the Project Launcher's collection/metadata automation to Guardian Studios' expected output formats (vertical explainer, podcast-video, journalist-led short).
Flag to MMR stakeholders that Guardian Studios + video podcast launch = new demand on LucidLink + iconik capacity within weeks of go-live.

decisionPanels.title: ⚠️ iconik interface overhaul is live — verify with your users now
decisionPanels.summary: iconik deployed a significant interface refresh from 4 March 2026: new navigation bar, grey background replacing the blue theme, admin functions consolidated into a dropdown menu, and the Upload button removed from the top bar (drag & drop and iconik Agent pushed as primary ingestion paths). Dark mode and a full design system rollout are planned throughout 2026. This is live in production for Guardian producers right now.
actions: Validate with multimedia producers that the new iconik nav and missing Upload button aren't causing confusion — especially in the first weeks of MMR go-live.
Update any training materials or producer guides that reference the old navigation or Upload button.
Monitor iconik changelog for the grey-background rollout phase and accessibility updates — the design system work will continue through 2026.

decisionPanels.title: Audience + distribution: manual beats automated on TikTok, NAB is AI week
decisionPanels.summary: Wildmoka data shows TikTok Drafts manual publishing can outperform automated schedulers by up to 60% reach. Meta's Graph API v25.0 is replacing legacy reach metrics with Page Viewer metric by mid-2026 — analytics dashboards will need updating. NAB Show 2026 is the industry's AI inflection point this spring: Backlight, Tedial, and Harmonic all leading with practical AI workflow automation. LucidLink Connect is getting industry-wide press (SiliconANGLE, SVG) — the market is watching Guardian's class of deployment.
actions: Advise multimedia social team: for high-priority TikTok content, consider manual publish from Drafts rather than relying on Wildmoka scheduler for maximum algorithmic reach.
Flag Meta API v25.0 Page Viewer metric change to whoever owns Guardian's social analytics dashboards — change is mandatory by mid-2026.
Monitor NAB 2026 announcements (April) for Backlight/iconik and LucidLink product reveals that may be relevant to MMR roadmap.

changes.summary: Tonight's dominant signal: Guardian Studios makes Ricky's MMR work internally strategic, not just operational. The iconik UI is already changed — producer training materials need updating now. Distribution intelligence tightens around manual-first TikTok and impending Meta metric changes.
